Right I agree. But you didn’t mention that “risk” until today’s post. I just think we might be getting too caught up into subjective, narrative driven feedback loops that tend to project certainty and extreme conviction when the outcome isn’t necessarily that certain. Hence why I stated a lot of this geo political context is very speculative and subjective.

This is also why I’ve been mentioned my counter argument, such as western “political division” and volatility making it tough for rooted conservative cultures to unite with US based on deeper values and ideology (especially without figuring out the EU situation). That’s certainly what has been hinted by calling it the “Holy Alliance 2.0” and you mentioned these cultural connections when you first introduced the concept.

Just seems like the most sensible approach would be for these states to stay independent and leverage both side’s weaknesses to their own benefit, and keep their options open.

I also think, which I know you spend a lot of time investing into these geo political narratives, that it shouldn’t take up such a large portion of your bandwidth, especially since it is so speculative. I’d rather have you invest your energy into teaching about Stochastic Volatility through more interactive educational means like videos/screen sharing/etc.

But it is your channel and I’m not trying to dictate how you run it, just providing some feedback.

Also, I have acknowledged your talent, and you get alot of praise here, so I’m not sure why I would have to acknowledge it constantly if what you said earlier is directed at me.

At least 10 members here call you “mommy” on a daily basis 😂

Your calls you have mentioned recently have been great. Is that a result of your ability to read the tape or put together these geopolitical narratives? I’d say it’s more of the former.

On March 12, 2023, the Board authorized each of the 12 Federal Reserve 
Banks (Reserve Banks) to establish and operate the BTFP.  The BTFP made 
funding available to eligible depository institutions to help ensure that banks had 
the ability to meet the needs of all their depositors.  Under the BTFP, each 
Reserve Bank made loans, on a recourse basis, to eligible depository institution 
borrowers for up to one year, receiving eligible U.S. government securities 
collateral in return.  The BTFP ceased extending credit on March 11, 2024.
